hypothesis: Augmenting NaviDiv with a 3D-conformer-aware adaptive constraint—penalizing generated molecules whose lowest-energy conformer pairwise RMSD to recent generations is <1.5 Å or whose 3D-pharmacophore fingerprint Tanimoto to recent generations is >0.75—will, in REINVENT4 singlet-fission design with the FORMED+GEOM3D prior (1000 RL iterations, 100 molecules/step), preserve scaffold and fragment diversity at levels comparable to the Combined 1D/2D constraints while producing a statistically significantly higher mean singlet-fission score after 1000 iterations (p < 0.05), thus reducing the observed diversity–optimization speed trade-off.
